# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

name: Rapid Build and Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main # Adjust to your primary branch (e.g., master, develop, release/*)

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Example for a Node.js application:
      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18' # Specify your Node.js version

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Use npm ci for faster, repeatable builds, without audit

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build # Replace with your specific build command (e.g., yarn build, grunt build)

      # Example for a Python application:
      # - name: Set up Python
      #   uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      #   with:
      #     python-version: '3.x' # Specify your Python version
      # - name: Install dependencies
      #   run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      # - name: Build application
      #   run: python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel # Or other build steps

      # Example for a Go application:
      # - name: Set up Go
      #   uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      #   with:
      #     go-version: '1.x' # Specify your Go version
      # - name: Download Go modules
      #   run: go mod download
      # - name: Build application
      #   run: go build -o myapp . # Replace myapp with your executable name

      # After building, save artifacts for deployment
      - name: Archive build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: |
            build/ # Adjust to your build output directory (e.g., dist/, target/, .)
            # Add any other files or directories needed for deployment

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build # This job depends on the 'build' job completing successfully
    environment:
      name: production # Or staging, development, etc. Define this in your GitHub repository settings
    steps:
      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: ./app-to-deploy # Directory where artifacts will be downloaded

      - name: Deploy application
        run: |
          echo "--- Starting rapid deployment to production ---"
          # Replace these with your actual deployment commands.
          # Examples:
          # - Deploy to S3:
          #   aws s3 sync ./app-to-deploy s3://your-production-bucket --delete
          # - Deploy to EC2 via SCP:
          #   scp -r ./app-to-deploy/* user@your-ec2-ip:/var/www/html/your-app
          # - Deploy to Kubernetes:
          #   kubectl apply -f ./app-to-deploy/kubernetes-manifests/
          # - Deploy to Azure Web App:
          #   az webapp deploy --name your-app-name --resource-group your-resource-group --src-path ./app-to-deploy
          # - Using a custom deployment script:
          #   ./app-to-deploy/deploy-script.sh
          
          echo "Placeholder for actual deployment commands."
          echo "Access artifacts in: ./app-to-deploy"
          echo "Deployment to production environment completed!"
        env:
          # Define environment variables needed for deployment (e.g., AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, KUBECONFIG)
          # These should be stored as GitHub Secrets in your repository or environment settings.
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          # KUBECONFIG: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG }} # Example for Kubernetes
          # AZURE_CREDENTIALS: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CREDENTIALS }} # Example for Azure
          # Add any other necessary secrets or variables here
